items.speaker: Chisa
items.text: Nice weather today. Rare to see a sunset like this. If Sumika were here, she'd probably capture this moment in one of her ambers.

options.text: Looking at it does take a weight off.

items.speaker: Chisa
items.text: No. To investigate what could possibly cause a sunset so unusual...

items.speaker: Chisa
items.text: See that child by the signpost?

items.speaker: Chisa
items.text: That's the seventh time I've seen him fall there.

items.speaker: Chisa
items.text: The cashier keeps wiping a stain that isn't there... The walls are barely standing, but her uniform is spotless... just like it was.

items.speaker: Chisa
items.text: The vending machine is due for restocking. With soda bottles that are already decades old.

items.speaker: Chisa
items.text: That cat has been lying there for two months... maybe longer. I've only known it for two.

items.speaker: Chisa
items.text: Middle school students are still taking photos of the sky after class, one that is now torn apart by Resonance Cords. But in their eyes, it's still clouds. Birds. Rainbows... the perfect sky of that single day.

items.speaker: Chisa
items.text: The store keeps playing pop songs from the last century. The window that never shut all the way is still stuck at the notch it was left on years ago.

items.speaker: Chisa
items.text: They've all been left behind in the past.

items.speaker: Chisa
items.text: As for me... I'm starting to forget what day it is today.

items.speaker: Chisa
items.text: This kind of life almost feels like...

options.text: An anime episode that got cut halfway?

items.speaker: Chisa
items.text: …Heh. Interesting analogy. When I was little, every summer break was spent with cool fans, watermelons, and an old TV. I loved watching anime and was always curious about those... special worlds.

items.speaker: Chisa
items.text: …Heh. Interesting analogy. When I was little, I once lost an entire holiday's worth of homework I'd worked really hard on. My parents and teachers didn't scold me, but I still felt terrible about it for a long time... Now I always save my work the second I finish it.

items.speaker: Chisa
items.text: I think it's kind of like... an unanswered call.

items.speaker: Chisa
items.text: My parents were always busy. Whenever I missed them, I'd try calling... but most of the time, they couldn't answer. Even when they did, we never had much to say.

items.speaker: Chisa
items.text: After I became a Resonator and was placed under supervision, those chances became even rarer. I think the things people said about me ended up putting a lot of pressure on them, too.

items.speaker: Chisa
items.text: In my hometown, Resonators aren't really accepted. Someone who can't control their abilities is like a ticking time bomb... feared and resented.

items.speaker: Chisa
items.text: I didn't want my parents to suffer because of me. So I thought... maybe if I stayed away, they wouldn't have to deal with the looks. Or the whispers.

items.speaker: Chisa
items.text: I can't say we're very close.

items.speaker: Chisa
items.text: ...It's not that I hate them. Things were just... ordinary. They had their jobs, I had school. We hardly spent time together.

items.speaker: Chisa
items.text: But... sometimes, I do miss them. Just a little.

items.speaker: Chisa
items.text: I really... really want to end this vicious loop.

items.speaker: Chisa
items.text: I've already tried so many times, but the outcome is always the same...

options.text: But you still haven't stopped trying.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Every time the Lament struck, you moved toward Neon Tower, against the tide of fleeing citizens, knowing full well it was the most dangerous place to be.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: You knew you couldn't save those who were already "gone," and still, you reached out. To the mother and daughter. To the injured young man. To me, when I first fell into this city.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: You knew that in this endless loop, saving someone didn't change a thing. But still, not once did you ignore a cry for help. So...

items.speaker: Chisa
items.text: So you appeared.

items.speaker: Chisa
items.text: At a time no one should have. In the most dangerous place. Beneath the Lament's shadow. It wasn't luck or coincidence.

items.speaker: Chisa
items.text: The Sonoro isn't fully sealed anymore. Sumika cracked it open. And from the moment we met, the loop had already begun to loosen.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Which means there's a chance to break it completely. I'm willing to put my faith in you. Let's seize that chance together.

items.speaker: Chisa
items.text: Why would you...

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Because we're partners. Isn't it only natural for me to trust you?

items.speaker: Namipon
items.text: Chisa—! Passenger—!

items.speaker: Namipon
items.text: I-I just confirmed Neon Tower's coordinates!
